description:
src/lib.rs:276 (get) and :386 (put) both return SecretError::NotFound on HTTP 404. The secretx-core contract is slightly ambiguous here — SecretError::Backend's doc lists 'the named secret was deleted' as a Backend example, but SecretError::NotFound exists as its own variant for exactly this purpose.

The choice to map 404 -> NotFound (not Backend) is correct because:
  - NotFound is a typed signal that allows callers to handle 'secret does not exist yet' as a non-error path (e.g., 'create on first put').
  - Mapping 404 -> Backend would lose this distinction and conflate 'secret missing' with 'permission denied', 'malformed request', etc.
  - Sibling backends (vault:206, doppler:155, aws-sm via SDK error matching) all map 404 -> NotFound.

A 'consistency with the Backend doc example' refactor that re-routed 404 to Backend would degrade the API. Defend.
title: Defend: 404 -> SecretError::NotFound mapping at call site
